notes: All 32 assertions pass (8/8) in both with_skill and without_skill configurations — assertions do not differentiate skill value on correctness.
The skill's real value is efficiency: 33% faster (56.9s vs 84.9s mean) and 23% fewer tokens (21,344 vs 27,600 mean).

Pomodoro-timer is the most discriminating eval: with_skill completed in 43s vs 113.7s without — a 2.6x speedup. This is because the skill provides the exact Block Artifactory registry URL, sharp rendering pattern, and render guard pattern up front, avoiding exploratory tool calls.

Window-layout-launcher shows the smallest efficiency gap (67.8s vs 78.1s, 21,874 vs 29,086 tokens) — AppleScript window management is fairly self-contained and less reliant on the Stream Deck-specific patterns the skill teaches.

The without_skill pomodoro run used @elgato-stream-deck/node v6.0.0 and sharp v0.33.0 (older versions) — the skill correctly pins v7.6.2 and v0.34.5 which are confirmed available on Block Artifactory.

Future assertion candidates that would better discriminate: render guard pattern present (rendering flag + lastRenderedKey), Block Artifactory registry URL in .npmrc or package.json, clearPanel() not called in poll cycle.
